core_model.structure_summary: A multi-species continuous-time Markov jump process (master equation) with transition rates that are bilinear in counts, solved through a probability generating function that obeys a first-order linear transport PDE.
core_model.canonical_form: \frac{dP_n}{dt} = \sum_i \big[ T(n\mid n+e_i)P_{n+e_i} + T(n\mid n-e_i)P_{n-e_i} - (T(n+e_i\mid n)+T(n-e_i\mid n))P_n\big], \quad \partial_t G = -n_0 m(z) G + \sum_i [(1-z_i)\nu_i + z_i m(z)]\,\partial_{z_i} G

variables.meaning: vector of nonnegative integer species counts
generating-function variable indexing the count distribution

deterministic_skeleton.summary: The characteristic ODE system of the generating-function transport equation, including a scalar Riccati equation dm/dt = -m^2 + Qm + const capturing the mean-field relaxation.

notes: Core is the multi-resource consumer-resource master equation (Markov jump process with bilinear rates). Its generating function solves a first-order linear PDE; the method-of-characteristics reduction yields an ODE system with a Riccati equation for m(t), which is the natural deterministic skeleton. Object_type could arguably be pde (for G) but the primary model is the stochastic_process/master equation.
